title: Summarize a passage in 3 bullets
response:
- Before the 1950s, cargo was loaded onto ships piece by piece in a slow, expensive process called break-bulk shipping, where loading a medium-sized ship could take a week and cost around $5.86 per ton.
- In 1956, American trucking entrepreneur Malcom McLean sent a converted tanker, the Ideal X, from Newark to Houston carrying 58 uniform metal boxes that could be lifted straight from truck to ship, dropping loading costs to roughly 16 cents per ton.
- The real breakthrough came through international standards agreed by 1968, which settled common container dimensions and twistlock corner castings so any crane in any port could handle any container, driving shipping costs so low that transport cost largely stopped determining where goods were made.
scoreDetail: fidelity: 9/10, coverage: 9/10, format compliance: 9/10, concision: 6/10 — Facts and coverage are accurate and complete, but each bullet is a long, clause-stuffed single sentence rather than tight prose.
